page: Amazon Web Services m5d.large is a general-purpose instance type belonging to the m5d family, utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 2 vCPUs (1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled) running at 3.1 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of 75 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results position the m5d.large in the lower performance tiers. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench is categorized as weak (bottom 25%), while multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the poor category (bottom 10%). For example, the Passmark CPU score is 1987.9, and the large-block memory read bandwidth is 13.25 GB/sec. Despite these lower-tier compute and memory metrics, the instance maintains a dedicated CPU allocation and benefits from direct-attached NVMe storage, which provides low-latency local scratch space compared to network-attached alternatives.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5d.large offers an entry-level general-purpose resource profile with the added value of bundled local NVMe storage. This makes it suitable for workloads requiring a balance of compute and memory alongside fast local temporary storage, such as small-scale databases, caching layers, web serving, and development environments. However, for highly intensive computational tasks or memory-heavy applications, the resource density and benchmark-per-resource tradeoffs may require scaling to larger instance sizes within the same family.
description: Amazon Web Services m5d.large is a general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor running at 3.1 GHz. It features 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the Nitro hypervisor. With a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, this instance provides a balanced resource profile. Benchmark data indicates entry-level performance, with single-core CPU metrics in the bottom 25% and multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics in the bottom 10%. The inclusion of dedicated local NVMe storage offers low-latency scratch space, making this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that benefit from fast local read/write capabilities without requiring massive compute power. The m5d.large is best suited for light general-purpose workloads, small databases, caching layers, and development environments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, suitable for light database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.large is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 75 GB local NVMe SSD storage on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A general-purpose virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, balanced DDR4 memory, and fast local NVMe SSD instance storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB DDR4 RAM
75 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD instance storage
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Dedicated CPU allocation with x86_64 architecture
Best suited for small databases, caching, and development environments
